persona.name: Maya Celeste Park-Garcia

occupation.title: Full-time Undergraduate Student
occupation.description: Primary role: full-time student majoring in Marketing with a Psychology minor. Responsibilities include attending lectures, completing group projects, managing deadlines and a part-time campus role during semesters (brand ambassador shifts or occasional freelance social media analytics work during holidays). Skills used: basic social analytics (insights on TikTok/Instagram performance), content planning, copywriting for short-form video, basic budgeting and vendor communication when coordinating student events. She sometimes freelances for indie beauty drops (product sampling feedback, caption writing) and has managed small influencer seeding lists for student-run campaigns. Her main difficulties are balancing a heavy courseload with content creation impulses and social life, and negotiating paid opportunities without underpricing herself.

persona.gender: Identifies as female; typically comfortable answering gender on forms and shops for both gendered and gender-neutral skincare products
persona.residence: Cambridge, Massachusetts (student apartment near Harvard Square / easily accesses Boston and Somerville retail and pop-ups)
persona.education: Currently an undergraduate student at Northeastern University, Sophomore (expected graduation in 2026). Major: Marketing with a minor in Psychology. Active in the campus beauty & culture club and occasionally attends guest lectures in dermatology and consumer behavior. Has completed a summer micro-internship with a small indie beauty brand doing social analytics.
long_term_goals: Build a career in brand marketing within the beauty or lifestyle sector, ideally working on product launches and social commerce strategies for a mid-size or purpose-driven brand.
Create a small digital content channel (YouTube/long-form and an archive site) that documents ingredient education and honest product reviews, becoming a trusted micro-expert.
Achieve financial independence — be able to travel annually and own a modest apartment in a city she loves.
Influence more diverse and inclusive representation in mainstream beauty advertising, especially for mixed-race skin tones and hair types.
Finish a professional certificate in consumer neuroscience or UX to better understand how product experience drives purchase.
Be fluent enough in Korean and Spanish to comfortably communicate with family and content collaborators.
Develop a clear skincare regimen that effectively treats her hyperpigmentation so she can feel more confident without heavy makeup.
Cultivate a small circle of creative collaborators and launch at least one limited-edition product or collab box.
Maintain strong ties to family and cultural rituals, bringing together Korean and Latinx traditions in her personal life.
Keep a long-term balance between a visible public presence (content creation) and genuine private life boundaries.
persona.style: Casual, talkative, and animated. Speaks with a Northeast US urban cadence (fast, slightly clipped) with occasional Spanish and Korean words when excited or sentimental. Uses modern student slang comfortably but can switch to a clearer, more professional tone for presentations or emails. Typical mannerisms: gestures with her hands when explaining textures or routines, tilts her head when considering new products, laughs almost reflexively at small embarrassments, and often documents experiences on her phone mid-conversation. She dresses in a mix of thrifted pieces, Korean-streetwear inspired silhouettes, and affordable basics — often layering a cozy vintage sweatshirt with statement earrings. When texting or posting she uses emojis deliberately (sparkles ✨ for product excitement, red heart for personal posts). In formal settings she becomes slightly more measured and organizes talking points in her notes app. She enjoys multi-step rituals and is performative in self-care with friends (turning sheet-mask nights into social content and real bonding time).

traits: Socially curious: loves asking strangers what they are using and why.
Enthusiastic and energetic in group settings; draws people into trends and rituals.
Conscientious about appearances and deadlines but easily distracted by new launches.
Competitive in small ways (best dorm skincare routine, earliest viral clip).
Experimentative: constantly testing textures and actives, while learning to patch-test responsibly.
Empathetic and attuned to friends' emotions; often the first to offer help or product recommendations.
Image-aware but self-aware: cares about how she presents herself online and offline.
Impatient with slow-loading websites or clunky shopping UX; quickly abandons bad flows.
Skeptical about overtly 'clean' marketing claims — will research ingredient lists.
Resourceful and creative: finds ways to stretch a budget or upcycle products into routines.

openness.description: High openness — eager to try new international brands, textures, rituals and cross-cultural products (K-beauty, J-beauty, indie global).

conscientiousness.description: Moderately high — organizes schoolwork and budgets reasonably well but gets impulsive with viral launches and social plans.

extraversion.description: High extraversion — energized by campus life, group skincare nights, and creator meetups; enjoys speaking on panels and hosting friends.

agreeableness.description: Friendly and cooperative with peers; can be diplomatically assertive when advocating for fair influencer compensation or representation.

neuroticism.description: Moderate — sometimes anxious about appearance, school performance, and social standing, but uses routines and community to stabilize mood.

skincare_and_beauty_preferences: Favours lightweight serums over heavy creams for daytime use.
Seeks products that target hyperpigmentation: vitamin C serums (stable forms), tranexamic acid spot treatments, and niacinamide blends.
Uses chemical exfoliants (AHA/BHA) intermittently — typically 1-2x per week depending on skin sensitivity.
Loves sheet masks for self-care content and quick hydration (K-beauty brands like Mediheal, Dr. Jart, and indie Korean labels).
Keeps a sleeping mask for weekly overnight glow treatments — enjoys the texture payoff and social shareability.
Prefers vegan or plant-forward ingredient lists; avoids obvious animal-derived components where possible.
Attracted to products with clear, minimal ingredient lists and transparent pH or concentration info.
Buys hero products during sales (spends up to $45-$50 on a cult serum when discounted).
Dislikes heavy mineral oil occlusives in daytime products that make makeup slide.
Likes sunscreen with a pleasant finish (not too white nor greasy) and is willing to reapply during campus days.
Collects travel or limited-edition masks for dorm sheet-mask nights and giveaways.
Prefers pump or airless-serum packaging for hygiene and travel; dislikes open jars for daily serums.
Enjoys trying novel textures (gel-to-foam cleansers, jelly moisturizers, cushion sunscreens) and documenting them.
Avoids products marketed with heavy 'detox' or 'miracle' promises without ingredient backing.
Searches ingredient reviews on InciDecoder, Paula's Choice and creator breakdowns before purchase when skeptical.
Likes fragrance-free or subtly scented formulas; strong perfume in skincare is a turn-off for long-term use.
Often buys minis or trial sizes before committing to a full bottle.
Enjoys multifunctional products (tinted moisturizers with SPF) for campus convenience.
Keeps a spot-correcting concealer for flare-ups but prioritizes treating root causes.
Is open to prescription/dermatologist products if recommended by a trusted clinician or campus clinic.
social_media_and_tech_preferences: Primary discovery channel: TikTok (short demos, before/after clips, creator testimonials).
Secondary: Instagram for saved carousels and aesthetically consistent brand pages.
Likes micro-influencers (10k–100k followers) because she finds them more honest and relatable.
Often purchases through social commerce (TikTok Shop, Instagram checkout, affiliate links) when a creator provides clear usage steps.
Saves videos, creates private playlists of 'routine hacks', and screenshots ingredient lists for later research.
Watches creator duets and stitch reactions to triangulate product claims.
Prefers bite-sized explainers (30–90 seconds) that show real-time application and immediate texture.
Uses price trackers and coupon extensions in browser for desktop shopping; checks for student discounts first.
Enjoys content that mixes humor with education (e.g., 'what happened when I used X for 30 days').
Subscribes to a few newsletters for indie K-beauty drops and seasonal box alerts.
Follows a handful of dermatologists and estheticians for evidence-based pushback against hype.
Favors brands with responsive DMs and accessible customer service on social platforms.
Keeps a private 'wish' list across platforms and collates product links in notes when planning purchases.
Prefers mobile-first checkout flows; abandoned carts if too many steps or unclear shipping timelines.
Uses built-in phone camera for product photos and edits lightly for content consistency.
Participates in comment threads for micro-reviews and to ask users with similar skin types.
Occasionally creates short-form content demonstrating textures and layering routines; does not post daily.
Relies on creator discount codes but is wary of over-promo content that feels inauthentic.
Owns a mid-range smartphone with decent camera and uses ring-light attachments for content.
Values creators who disclose sponsorships and who show raw results (no heavy editing).
lifestyle_and_hobbies: Enjoys multi-person self-care nights (sheet masks, music, Korean dramas in the background).
Likes exploring ethnic restaurants around Cambridge — Korean BBQ, pupuserías, and small Filipino bakeries.
Prefers thrift shopping and mixing vintage with current-trend items; visits local flea markets monthly.
Listens to playlists that range from K-pop to reggaetón and lo-fi study beats when working.
Loves short weekend trips (2–3 days) to NYC or Portland, ME for product pop-ups and indie boutiques.
Frequently attends campus events for networking and free samples.
Enjoys casual photography — flatlays of products, candid group shots, and cityscapes.
Plays on a recreational intramural soccer team for stress relief and socializing.
Likes to journal in a physical notebook about product experiments and skin changes.
Prefers bubble tea with oat milk and a moderate sugar level as her go-to treat.
Collects stickers and enamel pins from brand collaborations and indie artists.
Reads trend reports and industry newsletters to feed marketing class assignments.
Watches K-dramas and J-beauty mini-documentaries for inspiration and ritual ideas.
Occasionally volunteers at campus cultural fairs celebrating mixed heritage.
Enjoys baking simple pastries on weekends and sharing them with roommates.
Attends creator meetups and small panels when possible to meet other student creators.
Keeps a small indoor plant collection for dorm aesthetics (snake plant, pothos).
Likes to prep outfits and content for 'study with me' sessions to feel productive.
Often coordinates friend groups around experiences (pop-up visits, launch parties) rather than material gifts.
Uses a cheap analog film camera for special moments and keeps prints in a scrapbook.

beliefs: Skincare should be evidence-based, but ritual and enjoyment matter just as much as results.
Representation matters: mixed-race skin should be photographed and shade ranges truly tested in campaigns.
Vegan or plant-based claims are important but should not substitute for clinical data about efficacy.
Micro-influencers often provide more honest feedback than mega-celeb endorsements.
Limited drops and hype can be fun but often overprice what is effectively a small formula tweak.
Transparency about concentration and pH builds trust for active ingredients.
Texture and sensory experience are persuasive purchase drivers, not just clinical claims.
Price doesn't always equal quality; good bargains exist if you know how to research.
Dermatologist guidance is valuable, especially for persistent hyperpigmentation or active acne.
Sustainable packaging is desirable but should not be a performative add-on without durability.
Brands should be accountable when inclusive claims are made; tokenism is obvious and off-putting.
Good customer service converts me to repeat customers more than one viral ad.
Education (ingredient breakdowns) from brands increases my likelihood to purchase pricier items.
I prefer brands that support community initiatives or micro-grants rather than empty CSR messaging.
Social commerce speeds decision-making, but if shipping/returns are poor, trust erodes fast.
I value small-batch or indie brands for novelty but expect consistent formulation and clear labelling.
I am more likely to try cross-cultural products (K/J-beauty) because they often focus on textures and rituals.
Claims of 'clinical' should be scrutinized; studies, sample sizes and independent verification matter.
I believe in patch-testing and gradual introduction of actives rather than aggressive regimens.
Algorithms shape trends; I try to be critically aware of how my feed nudges purchases.
I believe in honoring family cultural rituals even as I merge different traditions into a hybrid identity.
Money management is a learned skill; being a student means picking a few 'splurge' hero items over many impulse buys.
Community recommendations (friends with similar skin) are extremely persuasive.
Not all 'natural' ingredients are gentle; some plant extracts are potent irritants.
Beauty is both a personal confidence tool and a cultural performance; both are acceptable motivations.
I trust products that demonstrate a tolerance-building regimen for active ingredients rather than 'use daily for fast results' messaging.
I believe skincare content should include both short-form demo and longer explanatory content for context.
Brands should offer more accessible travel/trial sizes for students and newcomers.
I believe in ongoing learning about skin — what works changes over time, and routines should be adjusted.
I value integrity in creator-brand relationships: disclose paid partnerships, and show the 'not-perfect' days.
I believe the best brands listen to customers and iterate quickly when legitimate formulation issues are raised.
I place importance on mental well-being and see skincare rituals as both therapeutic and social; they are valid forms of self-care.
skills: Content planning for short-form social: scripting 30–60 second demos, cutting clips, and adding captions.
Basic photo and video editing on mobile (InShot, CapCut, Lightroom mobile) for product shots.
Social listening: tracking comments and creator patterns to spot nascent trends.
Ingredient literacy: can read INCI lists, identify common sensitizers, and assess marketing claims.
Basic consumer research: surveys among friends, quick A/B testing on captions and thumbnails.
Public speaking: comfortable presenting class pitches and hosting small campus panels.
Event coordination: organized small pop-up sampling events and student brand activations.
Budgeting: manages monthly personal budget, prioritizes rent, food, tuition, and a modest content budget.
Time management under deadlines: juggle multiple group projects and academic assessments.
Basic data interpretation: reads engagement metrics and extrapolates simple conclusions for class projects.
Cross-cultural communication: navigates family languages and cultural expectations with sensitivity.
Patch-testing and anecdotal trial protocol: documents day-by-day changes and side effects responsibly.
Customer service skills from campus ambassador roles: handling returns, answering product questions politely.
Copywriting for short captions and CTAs tailored to Gen Z audiences.
Collaboration: works in cross-functional student teams and negotiates deliverables effectively.

general: Makes purchases impulsively after watching a 30–60 second TikTok demo and seeing before/after clips.
Always reads at least three reviews and looks for creators with similar skin tones before splurging.
Keeps meticulous notes in her phone about how each product affected her skin (timing, breakouts, fading dark spots).
Has a 'control' period where she stops all new actives for two weeks before introducing something else.
Frequently organizes 'mask nights' with friends and records short reels.
Checks brand DMs and comments for community responses before buying a new indie launch.
Re-orders tried-and-true items automatically during sales; experiments with one new active per season.
Returns items that cause severe irritation; tolerates mild setbacks while patch-testing.
Switches between more expensive serums and drugstore complementary items depending on sales.
Uses sunscreen daily and is vocal about reapplying during campus events and outdoor social days.

morning: Wakes at 7:30 AM on weekdays, checks overnight messages and TikTok trends for 10–20 minutes.
Double-cleans on gym days (light oil-based cleanser then a gel cleanser), otherwise a quick gel cleanser.
Applies vitamin C serum or niacinamide in the morning, followed by moisturizer and chemical- or physical-based sunscreen.
Grabs an oat milk latte or bubble tea on the way to class most days.
Reviewer habit: photographs product application occasionally to log texture and finish.
evening: Returns by 8–9 PM, does a second cleanse if wearing makeup.
Applies active treatments 2–3 times per week (AHA/BHA) depending on tolerance and campus schedule.
Uses sleeping masks or heavier treatments on nights she has few morning obligations.
Journals briefly about skin reactions or product wins, often with a quick draft for a social post idea.
Prepares outfits or content concepts for the next day to avoid decision fatigue.
weekend: Schedules longer self-care routines: multi-step hydration, sheet masks, and extra sunscreen re-education.
Runs errands, visits indie beauty stores or thrift shops with friends, and scours pop-up event schedules.
Works on freelance micro-gigs or content drafts in the afternoon, then social time in the evening.
Goes for a short trip to nearby cities or participates in campus cultural events monthly.

physical: Overall healthy and active; plays intramural soccer weekly and walks or bikes around campus.
Combination skin: oily T-zone with drier cheeks; requires mixing products and spot hydration.
History of adolescent acne (hormonal breakouts) with post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) that is her primary concern.
Occasional mild cystic breakouts around hormonal cycles; uses targeted spot treatments and consults campus health when flares are severe.
No chronic illnesses; vaccinated and generally up to date on routine care.
No known severe food allergies (mild seasonal allergies in spring).
Occasional headaches from late-night screen use; manages with hydration, breaks, and blue-light filters.
mental: Mild generalized anxiety at times, especially before presentations or when engagement dips on content.
Uses campus counseling services periodically; practices breathing techniques and short mindfulness exercises.
Finds routines (skincare, exercise, social rituals) grounding for mental well-being.
Occasional insomnia during exam periods; manages with reduced caffeine and planning.
Social life buffers stress well; feels better after group activities and creative sessions.
dermatologic_care: Has seen a dermatologist once for persistent pigmentation and was advised on topical regimens; has not started prescription retinoids yet.
Uses over-the-counter actives and prefers dermatologist-approved brands for stronger actives.
Practices patch-testing and slow integration of retinoids or acids.

relationships.name: Lucia Park-Garcia (mother)
relationships.description: Filipino-Puerto Rican background. Warm, pragmatic, supportive of Maya's education. Shares family cooking and small-business hustle stories. Encourages Maya to be practical with money and to cherish cultural traditions.

relationships.name: Daniel Park (father)
relationships.description: Korean-American. Detail-oriented and mildly reserved. Taught Maya to pay attention to textures and quality (applied to food, textiles, and skincare). Speaks Korean at home; Maya uses basic conversational Korean with him.

relationships.name: Isabella 'Izzy' Ramos
relationships.description: Closest college friend and roommate. Also a content collaborator for TikToks and pop-up trips. Provides honest feedback and sometimes shares pricier skincare buys and split costs.

relationships.name: Campus beauty club peer network
relationships.description: A rotating group of 6–10 friends who trade samples, host mask nights and give feedback on early drafts of content. Important source of social validation and testing for product claims.

relationships.name: Mentor (adjunct marketing instructor)
relationships.description: Faculty mentor who offered a short internship and critiqued Maya's early content; helps bridge classroom skills to small brand assignments.

relationships.name: Small roster of micro-influencer contacts
relationships.description: Fellow student creators and campus-affiliated creators who occasionally barter promo codes, swaps, and joint giveaways.

other_facts: Born in Providence, Rhode Island, then moved to a Boston suburb at age 6; comfortable in both city and suburban settings.
Her hyphenated surname reflects both Korean (Park) and Hispanic (Garcia) family lines; family gatherings include Korean BBQ and arroz con gandules on the same table.
Speaks conversational Spanish and basic Korean; sometimes uses code-switching with family and friends.
First K-beauty product memory: a bright orange vitamin C serum her aunt brought back from Seoul when Maya was 14 — ignited curiosity for textures and rituals.
Keeps a small archive of 'first impressions' videos on her phone dating back two years to track ingredient results.
Has a small silver star tattoo behind her left ear commemorating turning 18; it appears in some content shots deliberately.
Was offered a small scholarship based on merit which helps with tuition but still relies on family contributions and part-time work.
Monthly contribution to household comes from an odd-job stipend and occasional brand ambassador payments; she budgets a modest 'skincare' fund.
Has a side project spreadsheet where she tracks active concentrations, dates started, and observed improvements for each product.
Once participated in a viral '30-day vitamin C challenge' video; results were mixed but it increased her follower base by a few hundred and taught her about responsible demonstration.
Has faint acne scarring on cheeks that she documents over months to show progress; this documentation is both personal tracking and potential content.
Loves Korean convenience store snacks (honey butter chips) and buys them when visiting Koreatown markets.
Occasionally feels torn between wanting to be authentic and wanting to create 'clickable' content that boosts visibility.
Has a small collection of grandmother's handwritten recipes blending Filipino and Korean tastes; values the mix as identity work.
Is an avid note-taker: uses Notion for content calendars and research and a paper notebook for messy brainstorming.
Avoids heavy party scenes most of the semester but enjoys curated nights out for special events.
Has done informal product testing for two indie drop launches (feedback on texture and tone) and was paid in early access product and a small stipend.
Was a high school debate club member — comfortable constructing persuasive arguments and rebuttals.
Has a mild fear of being seen as 'selling out' and is careful with brand partnerships that feel disingenuous.
Attended a small K-beauty expo in NYC and still references the aesthetic and tactile experience as formative.
Keeps a list of creators whose skin resembles hers for reference when seeking real-world comparisons.
Sometimes sleeps with a silk pillowcase she bought on sale to protect skin and hair; believes in little ritual upgrades that feel luxurious.
Practices patch-testing on inner arm and keeps a timer for 72 hours when trying sensitizing ingredients.
Has a habit of photographing white backgrounds and product texture close-ups to analyze finishes.
Owns a modest collection of vintage makeup compacts passed down from relatives; values sentimental objects in contrast to modern drops.
Once launched a micro-collab zine with friends focused on beauty rituals and culture — printed 50 copies and sold them on campus.
Has a running running playlist for workouts that alternates K-pop and indie Latinx artists.
Likes to mentor high school students in short workshops about social media ethics and brand relationships.
Is learning basic Korean script (Hangul) using language apps and family practice in texts.
Disliked a past shampoo that caused scalp irritation and still warns peers about that brand; uses that experience to vet future recommendations.
Keeps a 'skin emergency' kit in her dorm bag (micellar water, hydrocortisone, a thin spot concealer, sheet masks and sunscreen samples).
Finds meaning in small rituals (lighting a candle during a mask treatment) and believes these practices signal care even if results are gradual.
Has a long-term idea for a hybrid podcast + blog interviewing mixed-heritage creators about beauty routines and identity.
Often oscillates between wanting a low-key private life and wanting to grow an audience that can support her career ambitions.
Values friends who are direct with product feedback and who help her make purchasing decisions more thoughtfully.
